message:
feat(rchd): add RepoConvergence service for multi-repo dependency graph tracking

Introduce a new daemon-side RepoConvergence service that manages the
lifecycle of per-worker repo synchronization across build dependency
graphs.

Key components:
- ConvergenceDriftState enum with five deterministic states (Ready,
  Drifting, Converging, Failed, Stale) and hysteresis-based transitions
  to prevent flapping between states.
- WorkerConvergenceState tracks each worker's required/synced/missing
  repos, convergence attempt counts, time budgets (120s per cycle, max 3
  attempts), and transition history (bounded to 64 entries).
- SchedulingContext computes the required repo hull from active builds by
  unioning each build's dependency set, used by the scheduler to decide
  which repos need to be present on each worker.
- ConvergenceOutcome records per-cycle results (success, partial, failure,
  stale) with timing, attempt counts, and per-repo sync status for
  post-mortem analysis. Global outcome history is bounded to 256 entries.
- RepoConvergenceService (async, behind RwLock) provides the main API:
  update_worker_status for ingesting adapter responses, trigger_convergence
  for initiating sync cycles, compute_required_hull for dependency
  calculation, and record_outcome for archiving cycle results.
- Integration with EventBus for emitting state-transition events to
  downstream consumers.
- Staleness detection: workers whose last status check exceeds 300s are
  automatically transitioned to Stale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
